reconstructed_sketch: From chapter context, one would predict 2.30 to be another do-not-grieve argument. One would NOT predict the universalising 'tasmāt' that closes 20 verses of imperishable doctrine, the bridge function to the duty argument at 2.31, or the engineering analog explicitly recapitulating the previous block (clothing/2.22, body-replacement/2.13, unmanifest-bracket/2.28) and naming the action-pivot. The verse contributes the closing-coda function specifically.
verse_specific_signal: The bridge function — closing the imperishable doctrine and pointing to the duty argument that begins next — is the contribution 2.30 makes that no other verse in the chapter makes. The engineering layer foregrounds this by naming the 'therefore' as the whole point of the verse and gesturing forward to 2.31.

overlap_with_other_verses.explanation: Both verses use the 'tasmāt... śocitum arhasi' structure, but 2.25 closes the inner imperishable cluster (2.20-2.25) while 2.30 closes the whole sāṅkhya block (2.11-2.30) and bridges to duty. Complementary, not duplicate.

verdict_reason: The bridge function is the verse's specific signal. The engineering layer preserves it through explicit recapitulation of the previous block and explicit foreshadowing of 2.31.
